title: Pulp project-import SPI v0
description: The JSON-over-stdio service-provider interface between the Pulp SDK (host) and an installed project importer (add-on tool). The SDK invokes the importer with one request envelope per line on stdin and reads one response envelope per line on stdout; stderr is human/log only. Four verbs: `detect` (is this a project I understand, and how confident?), `analyze` (produce a project-import IR), `plan` (turn an IR + target capabilities into an emission plan), `emit` (the IR/plan the SDK materializes — the tool returns the plan, the SDK WRITES the files). Version is negotiated: every message carries `spi_version`, and the registry pins each importer to an `spi_min`/`spi_max` window so a mismatch fails loudly ('upgrade Pulp' / 'upgrade importer') instead of silently misbehaving. Framework identity is runtime DATA throughout (`framework`/`vendor_id` strings); the SDK and this schema name no vendor.

version_range.description: An inclusive [min, max] SPI/SDK version window for negotiation.

request.description: SDK -> importer. One per line on stdin.

id.description: Correlates the response to this request.

response.description: importer -> SDK. One per line on stdout. `ok=false` carries `error`; diagnostics may accompany either outcome.

detect_candidate.description: A framework the importer recognises in the target dir, with confidence and evidence. Returned by `detect` even when the matching importer would be a different tool (so the SDK can print the install hint).

framework_path.description: The user's own framework checkout (read-only; never vendored).

target_caps.description: What the destination Pulp SDK can express, so the plan only commits to supported facts (e.g. whether shaped param ranges exist yet).

pulp_import_plan.description: The SDK-materialized emission plan: an ordered list of file actions plus the residual policy. The importer PROPOSES this; the SDK WRITES the files (the tool never writes the final project, except via an explicit unstable custom-emitter escape hatch).

from.description: Source path for copy actions (read-only user file).

template.description: SDK template id for generate/scaffold actions.

provenance.description: Drives license_provenance in the report and the clean-room output check.

emit_result.description: The `emit` verb's result: an EmissionManifest the importer PROPOSES and the SDK MATERIALISES. The importer never writes into the user's tree — every generated/stub file ships its `content` inline, every verbatim portable-core copy ships an absolute `copy_from` source path. The SDK writes each file under the request's `output_dir`, runs the clean-room output denylist scan over every `generated` file (a `copied-user-file` is the user's own code and is EXEMPT), and records provenance. `migration_status` here is the rich status document (the on-disk migration_status.json), distinct from the short enum in `emission_manifest`.

importer_id.description: Importer identity for the provenance marker (DATA, importer-owned).

output_dir.description: Informational echo of the requested output dir; paths are relative to it.

migration_status.description: The rich migration-status document the SDK writes to migration_status.json.

deferred_formats.description: Source formats not yet emitted, recorded so the info isn't lost.

unresolved.description: Labelled-stub / unresolved-construct notes carried into the scaffold.

emit_file.description: One file the importer proposes. Exactly one of `content` (generated/stub) or `copy_from` (verbatim copy of a framework-free user file) is set.

provenance.description: Drives the clean-room output scan: generated/stub are scanned for framework-source tells, copied-user-file is the user's own code and is exempt.

copy_from.description: Absolute source path for a verbatim copied-user-file.

emission_manifest.description: What the SDK actually wrote, returned/recorded after emit. Feeds the migration_status manifest + the `pulp ship` unresolved-scaffold gate.

verdict.description: e.g. 'Builds: yes. Audio parity: partial. UI parity: no. Session compatibility: no.'

compat_matrix.description: Per-feature supported / partial / unsupported map for a framework, sourced from the importer (content is package-owned; schema is SDK-owned). Powers the public per-framework honesty matrix.
